sources.title: The Runaway Bus
sources.description: Title: The Runaway Bus Summary: When heavy fog prevents all aircraft from leaving London airport, a group of passengers take an airline bus to get them to an alternative airport. However, one among their number is the ... Directed by: Val Guest Actors: Marianne Stone, Lionel Murton, Lisa Gastoni Production Company: Val Guest Productions Release Date: 8 October 1954 (Finland) Aspect Ratio: 1.37 : 1 When heavy fog prevents all aircraft from leaving London airport, a group of passengers take an airline bus to get them to an alternative airport. However, one among their number is the mastermind behind a bullion robbery at the airport... and particularly keen to escape the fog!

sources.title: THE RUNAWAY BUS video quality upgrade
sources.description: “Fun in the fog” “Frothy comedy” “A first class load of spooky fun” (IMDB reviews quotes). NOTE: This is the best print I’ve seen that also is in the correct aspect ratio. A classic comic mystery with lots of twists and turns mixed in with the laughs. Excellent British cast, good production values and definitely worth its 75 minutes. IMDB rating 6.3. Written and directed by Val Guest. Released in 1954. Complete print. CAST NOTE: The feature film debuts of Frankie Howerd, Belinda Lee and Michael Gwynn. TRIVIA: Frankie Howerd’s long telephone monologue was completely ad-libbed during the last hour of shooting, when it was discovered the film was too short to qualify as a main feature under British regulations.

metadata.Plot: When heavy fog prevents all aircraft from leaving London airport, a group of passengers take an airline bus to get them to an alternative airport. However, one amongst their number is the mastermind behind a bullion robbery at the airport... and particularly keen to escape the fog.
